mn30:0.2: The Shorter Simile of the Heartwood
mn30:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
mn30:10.1: But here, brahmin, a certain person has gone forth from the household life into homelessness out of faith:
mn30:10.10: Because of that accomplishment in ethical conduct, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:10.11: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in ethical conduct, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:10.13: He is pleased with that accomplishment in *samādhi*, and considers his aim fulfilled.
mn30:10.14: Because of that accomplishment in *samādhi*, he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn30:10.15: ‘I am endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhito*], with a gathered mind [*ekaggacitto*], while these other *bhikkhus* are not endowed with *samādhi* [*asamāhitā*], with distracted minds [*vibbhantacittā*].’
mn30:10.16: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in *samādhi*, he does not arouse desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, does not strive, and he is sluggish and lax.
mn30:10.17: Just like that man, brahmin, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, who came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, he cut off the bark, took it away, and departed thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:10.18: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.
mn30:10.19: It is to such a person, brahmin, that I compare this individual.
mn30:10.2: ‘I am overcome by birth, aging, and death, by sorrows, lamentations, *dukkha*, distress, and despair; overwhelmed by *dukkha*, overcome by *dukkha*.
mn30:10.3: Perhaps an ending of this whole mass of *dukkha* might be discerned.’
mn30:10.4: Having gone forth in this way, he acquires gains, honor, and praise.
mn30:10.5: He is not pleased with those gains, honor, and praise, and does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:10.6: Because of those gains, honor, and praise, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:10.7: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than gains, honor, and praise, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:10.8: He fulfills accomplishment in ethical conduct.
mn30:10.9: He is pleased with that accomplishment in ethical conduct, but does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:11.1: But here, brahmin, a certain person has gone forth from the household life into homelessness out of faith:
mn30:11.10: Because of that accomplishment in ethical conduct, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:11.11: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in ethical conduct, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:11.13: He is pleased with that accomplishment in *samādhi*, but does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:11.14: Because of that accomplishment in *samādhi*, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:11.15: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in *samādhi*, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:11.17: He is pleased with that knowledge and vision, and considers his aim fulfilled.
mn30:11.18: Because of that knowledge and vision, he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn30:11.19: ‘I dwell knowing and seeing, while these other *bhikkhus* dwell not knowing and not seeing.’
mn30:11.2: ‘I am overcome by birth, aging, and death …
mn30:11.20: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than knowledge and vision, he does not arouse desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, does not strive, and he is sluggish and lax.
mn30:11.21: Just like that man, brahmin, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, who came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, he cut off the softwood, took it away, and departed thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:11.22: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.
mn30:11.23: It is to such a person, brahmin, that I compare this individual.
mn30:11.3: Perhaps an ending of this whole mass of *dukkha* might be discerned.’
mn30:11.4: Having gone forth in this way, he acquires gains, honor, and praise.
mn30:11.5: He is not pleased with those gains, honor, and praise, and does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:11.6: Because of those gains, honor, and praise, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:11.7: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than gains, honor, and praise, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:11.8: He fulfills accomplishment in ethical conduct.
mn30:11.9: He is pleased with that accomplishment in ethical conduct, but does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:12.1: But here, brahmin, a certain person has gone forth from the household life into homelessness out of faith:
mn30:12.10: Because of that accomplishment in ethical conduct, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:12.11: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in ethical conduct, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:12.13: He is pleased with that accomplishment in *samādhi*, but does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:12.14: Because of that accomplishment in *samādhi*, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:12.15: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in *samādhi*, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:12.17: He is pleased with that knowledge and vision, but does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:12.18: Because of that knowledge and vision, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:12.19: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than knowledge and vision, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:12.2: ‘I am overcome by birth, aging, and death, by sorrows, lamentations, *dukkha*, distress, and despair; overwhelmed by *dukkha*, overcome by *dukkha*.
mn30:12.20: And what, brahmin, are the *dhammas* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision?
mn30:12.3: Perhaps an ending of this whole mass of *dukkha* might be discerned.’
mn30:12.4: Having gone forth in this way, he acquires gains, honor, and praise.
mn30:12.5: He is not pleased with those gains, honor, and praise, and does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:12.6: Because of those gains, honor, and praise, he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn30:12.7: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than gains, honor, and praise, he arouses desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, strives, and he is not sluggish or lax.
mn30:12.8: He fulfills accomplishment in ethical conduct.
mn30:12.9: He is pleased with that accomplishment in ethical conduct, but does not consider his aim fulfilled.
mn30:13.1: Here, brahmin, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome *dhammas*, a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], and *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
mn30:13.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:14.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the stilling of thought and examination [*vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, with internal clarity, with unification of mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], and with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi*.
mn30:14.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:15.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the fading away of *pīti*, a *bhikkhu* dwells in equanimity [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*sato*], fully aware [*sampajāno*], and experiences *sukha* with the body. He enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*, of which the noble ones declare: ‘One who dwells in equanimity [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*satimā*], dwells in *sukha*.’
mn30:15.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:16.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the giving up of *sukha* and the giving up of *dukkha*, and with the earlier disappearance of joy and distress, a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*, with purity of equanimity and *sati* [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*].
mn30:16.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:17.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the complete transcending of form-*saññās* [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the disappearance of resistance-*saññās* [*paṭighasaññānaṁ*], with non-attention to diversity-*saññās* [*nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā*], thinking, ‘Space is infinite,’ a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn30:17.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:18.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the complete transcending of the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘Consciousness [*viññāṇa*] is infinite,’ a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn30:18.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:19.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the complete transcending of the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn30:19.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:2.1: Then the brahmin Piṅgalakoccha went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to him, he exchanged greetings with the *Bhagavā*.
mn30:2.10: “Yes, sir,” the brahmin Piṅgalakoccha replied to the *Bhagavā*.
mn30:2.2: After exchanging courteous and cordial conversation, he sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, the brahmin Piṅgalakoccha said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn30:2.3: “Master Gotama, these ascetics and brahmins who are leaders of orders, leaders of groups, teachers of groups, well known, famous, founders of sects, regarded as sādhu by many people—
mn30:2.4: that is to say, Pūraṇa Kassapa, Makkhali Gosāla, Ajita Kesakambala, Pakudha Kaccāyana, Sañcaya Belaṭṭhaputta, and Nigaṇṭha Nāṭaputta—
mn30:2.5: did all of them directly know in accordance with their own claims, or did none of them directly know, or did some directly know and some not?”
mn30:2.7: whether all of them directly knew in accordance with their own claims, or none of them directly knew, or some directly knew and some did not.
mn30:2.9: Listen to it, apply your mind carefully, and I will speak.”
mn30:20.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the complete transcending of the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn30:20.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:21.1: Furthermore, brahmin, with the complete transcending of the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the cessation of *saññā* and *vedanā* [*saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ*], and having seen with discernment [*paññāya*], his *āsavas* are exhausted.
mn30:21.2: This too, brahmin, is a *dhamma* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:21.3: These, brahmin, are the *dhammas* higher and more refined than knowledge and vision.
mn30:22.1: Just like that man, brahmin, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, who came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. He cut off just the heartwood, took it away, and departed knowing, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:22.2: And he would experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.
mn30:22.3: It is to such a person, brahmin, that I compare this individual.
mn30:23.1: So, brahmin, this holy life is not lived for the sake of gains, honor, and praise, nor for the sake of accomplishment in ethical conduct, nor for the sake of accomplishment in *samādhi*, nor for the sake of knowledge and vision.
mn30:23.2: But this unshakable liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*], brahmin—
mn30:23.3: this is the purpose of this holy life, brahmin, this is its heartwood, this is its culmination.”
mn30:24.1: When this was said, the brahmin Piṅgalakoccha said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn30:24.3: Let Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from today for life.”
mn30:24.4: The Shorter Simile of the Heartwood is finished, the tenth.
mn30:24.5: The Chapter on Similes is finished, the third.
mn30:25.1: Moḷiya, Phagguna, and Ariṭṭha by name,
mn30:25.2: At Andhavana, Questions, Puṇṇa, and The Bait;
mn30:25.3: The Heap, Kaṇeru, and the Great Elephant by name,
mn30:25.4: And again the Heartwood Simile with Piṅgalakoccha.
mn30:3.1: “Suppose, brahmin, a man wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, were to come upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, bypassing the bark, bypassing the rind, he would cut off the branches and leaves, take them away, and depart thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:3.2: A clear-sighted person, seeing him, would say this:
mn30:3.3: ‘Surely this good man did not know heartwood, did not know softwood, did not know bark, did not know rind, did not know branches and leaves.
mn30:3.4: And so this good man, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, bypassing the bark, bypassing the rind, he cut off the branches and leaves, took them away, and departed thinking, “Heartwood.”
mn30:3.5: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.’
mn30:4.1: Or suppose, brahmin, a man wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, were to come upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, bypassing the bark, he would cut off the rind, take it away, and depart thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:4.2: A clear-sighted person, seeing him, would say this:
mn30:4.3: ‘Surely this good man did not know heartwood, did not know softwood, did not know bark, did not know rind, did not know branches and leaves.
mn30:4.4: And so this good man, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, bypassing the bark, he cut off the rind, took it away, and departed thinking, “Heartwood.”
mn30:4.5: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.’

mn30:5.4: And so this good man, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, he cut off the bark, took it away, and departed thinking, “Heartwood.”
mn30:5.5: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.’
mn30:6.1: Or suppose, brahmin, a man wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, were to come upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, he would cut off the softwood, take it away, and depart thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:6.2: A clear-sighted person, seeing him, would say this:
mn30:6.3: ‘Surely this good man did not know heartwood, did not know softwood, did not know bark, did not know rind, did not know branches and leaves.
mn30:6.4: And so this good man, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, he cut off the softwood, took it away, and departed thinking, “Heartwood.”
mn30:6.5: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.’
mn30:7.1: Or suppose, brahmin, a man wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, were to come upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. He would cut off just the heartwood, take it away, and depart knowing, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:7.2: A clear-sighted person, seeing him, would say this:
mn30:7.3: ‘Surely this good man knew heartwood, knew softwood, knew bark, knew rind, knew branches and leaves.
mn30:7.4: And so this good man, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. He cut off just the heartwood, took it away, and departed knowing, “Heartwood.”
mn30:7.5: And he would experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.’
mn30:8.1: In the same way, brahmin, here a certain person has gone forth from the household life into homelessness out of faith:
mn30:8.10: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.
mn30:8.11: It is to such a person, brahmin, that I compare this individual.
mn30:8.2: ‘I am overcome by birth, aging, and death, by sorrows, lamentations, *dukkha*, distress, and despair; overwhelmed by *dukkha*, overcome by *dukkha*.
mn30:8.3: Perhaps an ending of this whole mass of *dukkha* might be discerned.’
mn30:8.4: Having gone forth in this way, he acquires gains, honor, and praise.
mn30:8.5: He is pleased with those gains, honor, and praise, and considers his aim fulfilled.
mn30:8.6: Because of those gains, honor, and praise, he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn30:8.7: ‘I am one with gains, honor, and praise, while these other *bhikkhus* are unknown and insignificant.’
mn30:8.8: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than gains, honor, and praise, he does not arouse desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, does not strive, and he is sluggish and lax.
mn30:8.9: Just like that man, brahmin, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, who came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, bypassing the bark, bypassing the rind, he cut off the branches and leaves, took them away, and departed thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:9.1: But here, brahmin, a certain person has gone forth from the household life into homelessness out of faith:
mn30:9.10: Because of that accomplishment in ethical conduct, he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn30:9.11: ‘I am ethical, one of good *dhamma*, while these other *bhikkhus* are unethical, of bad *dhamma*.’
mn30:9.12: And as for the other *dhammas* that are higher and more refined than accomplishment in ethical conduct, he does not arouse desire for the direct realization of those *dhammas*, does not strive, and he is sluggish and lax.
mn30:9.13: Just like that man, brahmin, wanting heartwood, seeking heartwood, searching for heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, who came upon a great standing tree possessed of heartwood. Bypassing the heartwood, bypassing the softwood, bypassing the bark, he cut off the rind, took it away, and departed thinking, ‘Heartwood.’
mn30:9.14: And he would not experience the benefit that is to be had from heartwood.
mn30:9.15: It is to such a person, brahmin, that I compare this individual.

mn30:9.3: Perhaps an ending of this whole mass of *dukkha* might be discerned.’
